request.system: Extract the keyed areas on these pages: description, encounters, treasure, connections, source pages, and a self-assessed confidence in [0, 1] per area.

parts.text:
[page 8]
The Town and Surroundings The Chaotic Caves
Town Key
The town lies at the crossroads of a river and
an old paved highway. It thrives on trade and
commerce. Several smithies provide weapons
and body armor. Merchants offer food,
clothing, and basic goods. A Cleric and healer
plies her wares. Horses and wagons are
available, as are freight, coach, and courier
services. Near the bridge are docks with a
narrow wooden pier and storage shed where
boats might be purchased or rented.
An irregular wooden stockade 15' high
surrounds most of the town, thrown up
haphazardly as it expanded. This is slowly
being replaced by a heavy stone wall about the
same height and ten feet thick, with
battlements occupying the top five feet. The
woods to the northwest of the town are being
cleared to allow the expansion of the walls.
The GM can add residents and NPCs as needed.
Additional residences and farms would be
scattered around outside the walls.
To save space, XP values are not given for the
villagers. Most are Normal Men, worth 10 XP
each. The GM can look up outliers if needed.
East, North and South Gates: These heavy
wooden gates can be barred from the inside.
Each has a hut beside it for a watch of three
soldiers from the garrison (see #38 for basic
troops). The east gate opens onto a dirt road
leading east towards farms and forest. A
cobblestone highway winds through the town
from the north to south gate.
Orchard: Apples, pears, pecans, etc. grow here.
It is the common property of the town.
1. HUNTERS AND DOG BREEDERS RESIDENCE:
These hunters also breed hunting and guard
dogs. They have several large pens for dogs.
Father: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 spear or 
1 shortbow, Dam 1d6, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐
2 Sons: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 spear or 
1 shortbow, Dam 1d6, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7
HP 3 ☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
2. CHICKEN RANCHERS AND HOME: These
ordinary people raise numerous chickens for
eggs, meat and feathers. They have coops as
well as a fenced-in area that the chickens can
get in and out of – and flee to if chased by
dogs.
Widow Woman: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), 
#At 1 kitchen knife, Dam 1d4, Mv 40', Sv NM, 
ML 7
HP 3 ☐☐☐
2 Older Children: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), 
#At 1 club, Dam 1d4, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7
HP 3 ☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
3. FAMILY RESIDENCE: This two-story home is
heavily built from large oaken beams. The top
story overhangs the bottom one. The
occupants do odd work for a living.
Father: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6+2 (bonus for strength of 16), Mv 40', 
Sv NM, ML 7
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐
5 Sons: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6+2 (bonus for strength of 16), Mv 40', 
Sv NM, ML 7
HP 3 ☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
3 ☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
2 ☐☐
4a. SMITH: This man has two apprentices who
help tend the forge. They spend a good deal of
time making nails, tacks, and other items. The
smith mainly makes iron hardware, from hinges
and fireplace pokers to iron railings for those
who can afford them.
Smith: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 hammer, 
Dam 1d6 +1 (bonus for strength of 14), Mv 40', 
Sv NM, ML 7
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐
2 Apprentices: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 
hammer, Dam 1d6, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7
HP 3 ☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
6

[page 22]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
The Caves
The GM has to decide where the caves are on
the map. They might even be scattered about
the countryside in different places. As drawn,
the cave map is wooded with occasional
clearings, but the GM may of course change
that. 
The monsters are trying to avoid leaving tracks
and trails so as to keep their lairs undetected.
Wild animals are few in the general area
around the caves; monsters are hunting them
pretty hard. As the characters get close to an
entrance, they will note it or the guards (if any)
on duty. Perhaps smoke from someone
cooking a meal in the open is seen rising above
the treetops, or is smelled by the PCs in the
breeze.
An adventuring party blundering around
outside the caves, making a lot of noise, should
alert the denizens to their presence; the
monsters will surely come out in force after
dark, if an adventuring party is foolish enough
to camp nearby.
The underground areas are not lit unless
otherwise noted. It is usually dead-silent
underground, making a Thief's ability to Move
Silently handy. Contrast that to when the wind
is blowing outdoors and the sound of the
leaves makes a constant background noise.
Monsters can hear PCs as easily as PCs can
hear monsters, so unless a monster is on guard
duty or hunting, it does not have an increased
chance of surprise versus PCs.
Most ceilings are 10 to 15 feet high, but the GM
should choose heights as desired. In some
places they are obviously higher, and in others
the GM might want to lower them.
Most of the doors to rooms are heavy wooden
doors that open with a simple latch and can be
barred from inside the room.
Steps typically have a 1 to 1 grade, going down
1 foot for each foot of length (so 10 foot long
steps drop 10 feet in elevation).
Exact elevations are not given to avoid fooling
the GM into thinking everything is neat and
orderly. The contour lines are only rough
guidelines for slopes. The region may look flat
on paper, but even the "flat" parts undulate.
Two entrances (or other features) that appear
to be on the same level could differ in
elevation by 10 to 20 feet. The tunnels will
constantly slope up and down.
Impress the irregularity of the ground from the
start to reduce "flat-world" thinking. If
someone starts arguing that part "A" doesn't
work because it looks like it is on the same
level as part "B", just tell them the ground
slopes up or down as needed and get on with
playing.
A. ORC LAIR
These orcs share (along with those at lair B
below) the duty of guarding the local orc
temple from intruders. They are brash and
haughty, prone to cruelty, tricks, and nastiness.
There is a chance (a 1 or 2 on 1d6) per hour (6
turns) that the player characters will
encounter 1d6 orcs moving about.
1d6 Wandering Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, 
#At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, 
XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
1. ENTRANCE: Two orcs usually stand guard
outside this gaping entranceway, leaning on
their spears. Others will be inside the first
chamber, which is their usual bunk room. Each
has 1d6 sp.
6 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
2. EMPTY ROOM: The walls and some of the
ceiling of this room collapsed some time ago.
It is currently unfit to live in.
3. COMMON ROOM: These are the quarters of a
number of orcs, with their weapons and gear.
Each has 1d6 sp. Crude furniture is in the
center of the room, and wooden bunks line the
walls. In the back are sacks of food stuffs
20

[page 23]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
(potatoes, etc.) and hanging meat (12 – 72
pounds each of veggies and meat).
8 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4. WOLF PEN. Both ends of this area are
blocked by a wooden fence spanning the
complete width and height of the halls; in the
center of each is a wooden gate about 3' wide
and 6' high, lashed shut by rope. Inside are
three wolves. They are usually alert and will
set up a clamorous barking and howling if they
hear or smell careless parties, resulting in a
chance of attracting orcs to see what is going
on (use the wandering orc concept explained at
the start of the lair for the chance and number
of orcs). Make a "hear noise" roll to see if the
jailers in #5 below hear and investigate as well.
The soft earthen floor of the room is littered
with wooden bowls full of water, bones, and
offal, but nothing else.
3 Wolves: AC 13, HD 2, #At 1 bite, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 60', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
7 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
5. DOOR TO JAIL: Behind this door are the
quarters for the big, sweaty orc who serves as
the warden of the jail (and also chief torturer)
and two assistants. The key to the jail door is
usually hung on a hide cord on the wall. Crude
furniture and various simple wooden or stone
interrogation devices fill the room. The warden
has 1d6 gp and the other two orcs 1d6 sp.
Orc Warden: AC 14, HD 2, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+1 for strength, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, 
XP 75
HP 10 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
2 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐ 
JAIL AREA: The locked door to the east opens
into the jail, where anyone captured will be
held. Currently there is a merchant who is a
normal man (bedraggled and missing a finger,
which was sent off with a ransom note). If
saved, he will give the party what he has at his
home (100 gp; it is obvious the orcs think he's
richer than he is). 
Merchant: AC 11, HD ½ (1d4 HP), #At 1 fist, 
Dam 1d3, Mv 40', Sv NM, ML 7, XP 10
HP 3 ☐☐☐
6. CHIEF'S ROOM: The strong, shrewd leader of
the orcs resides here, sitting on a large,
heavily-built throne when judging court. The
sub-room to the south contains a bed and
other personal furniture. His pets are also
here: a mountain lion and two wolves. The
tawny cat wears a collar with gems (100 gp
value for the gems) that is connected to a
strong steel chain. The chain is usually
attached to a steel hoop on the base of the
throne; the chain can be released quickly. The
wolves wear ordinary heavy leather collars, and
are usually chained to the wall beside the door.
Usually there are four orcs: two are here on
permanent duty and two more to serve as
messengers and go-fers. The chief has the key
to the west temple door (see room #9).
Orc Chief: AC 15, HD 4, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+1 due to Strength, Mv 20', Sv F4, 
ML 8, XP 240
HP 18 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Mountain Lion: AC 14, HD 3+2, #At 2 claws, 
1 bite, Dam 1d4 claw, 1d6 bite, Mv 50', Sv F3, 
ML 8, XP 145
HP 14 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
2 Wolves: AC 13, HD 2, #At 1 bite, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 60', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
4 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
21

[page 24]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
The throne is the treasure chest. There is a
silk pillow on it which is worth 200 gp. Under
the flip-up seat is: 671 gp, 1,235 sp, and 785 cp.
A stoppered flask in a padded jar contains a
Potion of Passwall – which replicates the
effect of the 5th-level Magic-User spell of that
name when poured onto an appropriate
surface.
7. COMMON ROOM/GUARD ROOM: Several orcs
are housed here as a guard post against those
in lair B. Each has 1d6 sp. The room is
cluttered with crude furniture and common
foods stuffs, some in bags, some in the form of
dried meats hanging on strings.
4 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
8. POOLS: These pools of water are fed by
underground springs. They provide a yearround source of fresh water. They are 6' deep
and clean, but otherwise not unusual.
9. SHARED HALLWAY: At each end of this
hallway is a heavy, thick wooden door bound
by strong steel bands, with crafty and heavy
locks. Each of the orc chiefs (rooms #6 and
#13) has the key to the opposite side. They
must cooperate to enter the temple.
Set here as temple guards, the orcs on either
side have been fighting so savagely for the
affections of the god in the temple that a high
priest had the doors locked. She gave the keys
to the chiefs, with the admonishment that any
further monkey business was not going to be
tolerated. 
10. ORC TEMPLE: This temple was carefully
cut from the living stone and is wellmaintained. The four pillars are fluted and
ornate; the floor is tiled with polished green
and red marble. The ceiling sweeps up some
20' high. On a dais some 6' above the floor is
the statue of a vindictive, evil orc god. It is
carved from a single shining piece of black
basalt, standing 12' high. It has two red gems
for eyes (each worth 250 gp). Steps are not
provided for getting atop the dais; no one is
supposed to stand on it.
B. ORC LAIR
These orcs share (with those at lair A above)
the duty of guarding the local orc temple from
intruders. They have a cruel sense of humor.
There is a 1-2 on 1d6 chance per hour (6 turns)
that the player characters will encounter 1d6
orcs while exploring the caves.
1d6 Wandering Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
ENTRANCE: Four orcs are immediately inside in
the first chamber, along with a hobgoblin that
went looking for greener pastures and latched
onto the orcs. Each has 1d6 sp.
4 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
Hobgoblin: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, Dam 1d8,
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
11. SECRET SAFE ROOM: Only the orc chief in
room #13 knows of this secret room. Both of
the secret doors that enter into it are so wellhidden that even close scrutiny has a low
chance of finding them. Each is also trapped
in two ways: when opened without disarming
the traps, (1) a bladder of gas will release,
causing anyone within 10' to make a save vs.
Poison or fall asleep for 1d6 hours. And (2) an
alarm bangs away loudly for a minute in the
orc chiefs quarters. Stashed in the main
chamber is a sword, a pouch with 25 gp, food,
and flint and steel, a skin of water, a cot, and a
cloak.
12. GUARD POST: Two orcs with spears and
hand axes stand on guard duty at this door.
Each has 1d6 sp. The short corridor between it
and the next room provides a buffer zone in
case of attack.
2 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear or 1 hand axe, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
22

[page 25]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
13. CHIEF'S QUARTERS: The wily orc chief
resides here. A war leader and a couple of
common orcs are also usually found here.
The chief has a key that opens the eastern
temple door at room #9 attached to a necklace
of long pencil-thin gold bars (value 100 gp).
Treasure is housed in a locked chest with a gas
trap inside it. The trap is released when it is
opened, unless some long pencil-sized object
is inserted into a hole in back (the chief's
necklace is used for this). The gas will affect a
10' area around the chest (save vs. Poison or
die; alternately the GM may substitute a
similarly-nasty effect of his or her own
devising). In the chest are 800 gp, 500 ep,
700 sp, and a Potion of Healing.
This room contains various bits of stout
wooden furniture. Under a heavy chair in the
northeast corner is the alarm bell that rings if
either of the trapped secret doors in room #11
is opened without disarming it first.
Orc Chief: AC 15, HD 4, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+2, Mv 20', Sv F4, ML 9, XP 240
HP 18 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Orc War Leader: AC 14, HD 2, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+1, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 9, XP 75
HP 10 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
2 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8 (9 while chief and/or leader
are present), XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
14. COMMON ROOM: This trash-littered room
houses nine orcs. They are currently throwing
rocks at one who is standing against a wall.
Several others are bruised. The game is to see
who can take the most beating before
flinching. Each has 1d6 sp.
9 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐
15. COMMON ROOM: This room houses seven
orcs. It is cluttered by miscellaneous debris
that they have torn up and discarded, as well
as firewood and stone circles for cooking.
Hidden in a hole in the northeast corner
(covered by a stack of fireplace rocks and a
foot of earth) is a bag with 10 pp and 400 sp.
7 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐
C. HOBGOBLIN LAIR
These hobgoblins have taken over what was
going to be an expansion of the funeral crypt at
D and are renovating it. The interior is a
mixture of smooth stone where the original
carvers completed their work, rough unfinished
work where they left off, and some work by
the hobgoblins.
ENTRANCE: Twin entrances lead into the first
chamber where five hobgoblins are bunked on
guard duty. Each has 1d6 sp.
At "A" is a wide underground stream being
used as a moat. Wooden planks about 15' long
(used to cross it) are stored on the opposite
side. A loud horn (from an ox) hangs on a peg
and can be blown to attract someone to come
put planks across (the hobgoblins at #17 are
tasked for the job). Other methods can be
used to cross it; if the players engage in a
lengthy debate or over-elaborate plan to cross,
roll 1d6 and on a 5 or 6 two hobgoblins from
#17 appear to interrupt.
In the northwest corner at "C" is a crude hide
painted to resemble stone used for laying
ambushes (i.e. hiding behind the hide and
letting people get inside the room, then rushing
out to attack).
5 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
23

[page 26]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
16. TRAPPED HALLWAY: Three sets of pillars
help support the 20' high ceiling of this
otherwise innocuous hallway. The hobgoblins
have strung thin wires along the floor between
the center two. Anyone tripping the wires will
be attacked by a heavy stone-headed mallet
swinging down from behind the pillars (it
attacks as a 2 HD creature and does 1d8 points
of damage if it hits) followed a moment later
by a net covered in bells that covers the hall
between the two pillars. Anyone caught under
the net will be tangled for 1d6 rounds and their
struggles will cause the bells to ring loudly.
There is a good chance hobgoblins from room
#17 below will hear the bells and come to
investigate.
17. COMMON ROOM: Six hobgoblins bunk here.
Each has 1d6 sp. They are not wearing armor
but will don it if given time. They have fixed
the place up, and are using what was once
going to be platforms for caskets as benches
and tables. Three fancy, elaborate red funeral
shrouds with thin gold threading and black
details are being used as hammocks, each
worth 50 gp.
6 Hobgoblins: AC 14 armored, 11 unarmored, 
HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, Dam 1d8, Mv 30' armored 
or 40', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
18. STORE ROOM: Usually this room is locked
(but not very well – a Thief could pick either
door with ease and the doors can be knocked
in easily). The chief (in #21) has the key to the
doors. It is full of odds and ends and minor
plunder: clothing, sacks of grains and potatoes,
hanging dried meats, perhaps some hanging
"fresh meat" in the form of slain foes. Also
there are mining tools: picks, chisels, hammers,
etc.
19. SUB-LEADER: This room has been taken
over by a skilled and tough hobgoblin, who
lives in luxury. On the wall are a short bow
and two quivers of arrows, as well as other
items. He has two pouches on his belt
containing 300 ep in one and 300 gp in the
other. He carries the key to this room on a
string made of hair on his belt.
Hobgoblin: AC 14, HD 3, #At 1 scimitar or 
1 shortbow, Dam 1d8 scimitar, 1d6 shortbow, 
Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 8, XP 145
HP 15 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
20. GUARD ROOM: The hobgoblins here have
piled up rubble in the hallway and stockpiled
six vials of holy water to fend off the ghouls to
the west. Each has 1d6 sp.
4 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
21. CHIEF'S ROOM: This chamber contains the
hobgoblin chief and 3 bodyguards. The chief
has the key to the store room at #18. There is
a plain bed, chairs, tables, and other
furnishings here.
A collection of goods on one table includes a
vial with a Potion of Stone to Flesh (1 use in it).
A bag tied to the foot of the bed holds 600 gp,
1,200 sp, and 1,800 cp.
Hobgoblin Chief: AC 15, HD 5, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F5, ML 8, XP 360
HP 25 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐☐☐☐
3 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8 (9 while chief is 
present), XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
D. ABANDONED CRYPTS
These are the old funeral crypts, which were
once going to be expanded at lair C. Once
highly decorated, the facade to the entrance is
now in ruin, with much of the marble overlay
broken off and scattered about, leaving bare
stone. A plaque that once held the name of
the crypts lies in shards on the ground. Bits of
bone, scraps of cloth, and chunks of wood
(some of it varnished) litter the floor and
clutters every floor inside.
In addition to the larger areas, there are five
small, rounded alcoves along the walls (two
east, three west) which contain pedestals for
shrines.
24

[page 27]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
22. GHOULS: Three ghouls lurk here trying to
get through the rubble to the hobgoblins in
#20. They are not interested in skeletons or
zombies as a possible food source.
3 Ghouls: AC 14, HD 2*, #At 2 claws, 1 bite, 
Dam 1d4 claw, 1d4 bite, all plus paralysis, 
Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 9, XP 100 ea.
HP 9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
9 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐
23. SKELETONS: This space contains six
skeletons (three on the east and three on the
west). They stand like guards stiff at
attention, and attack intruders on sight. The
area is cluttered by broken coffins. The
rubbish contains a silver dagger, 30 gp, and 21
pp.
6 Skeletons: AC 13 (½ damage from edged 
weapons, 1 point only from arrows or bolts), 
HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, Mv 40', Sv F1, 
ML 12, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
24. ZOMBIES: Zombies wait patiently here,
under orders to attack and destroy any
intruders. The area contains empty (old and
rotten) or smashed coffins. Amid the splinters
of what appears to have been an ornate
cabinet are six intact vials of holy water and
56 gp.
4 Zombies: AC 12 (½ damage from blunt 
weapons, 1 point only from arrows or bolts), 
HD 2, #At 1 bludgeon, Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F2, 
ML 12, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
25. SKELETONS: The skeletons posted here
attack on sight.
The outward facing walls are lined by crypt
spaces (3 high, 3 wide per 10' section); many
are empty. Those with anything in them have
the remains of poor people and (in total) little
more than 26 gp and 49 sp. Hanging out the
gaping square of one (in plain sight) is a
necklace with gems worth 750 gp; the original
owners will be so glad to have it recovered (as
a sentimental heirloom) that they will pay
1,000 gp for it, if they are tracked down.
6 Skeletons: AC 13 (½ damage from edged 
weapons, 1 point only from arrows or bolts), 
HD 1, #At 1 weapon, Dam 1d6, Mv 40', Sv F1, 
ML 12, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
E. GOBLIN LAIR
These goblins are living the simple life and
serving as spear fodder for the stronger tribes,
who aren't above recruiting them (by force) for
battles. Their crudely tunneled-out warren is
dark and dank. Many halls are 6' to 8' wide
and just as high.
26. GUARD POST: Six goblins hunker here,
ready to fight intruders. Each has 1d6 cp. They
control a net trap which they can drop by
jerking on a rope (it covers the trap area
marked on the map at the entrance). Entangled
opponents will be easily subdued, but can get
free in 1d6 rounds if they are left alone. The
GM should use common sense and an easilymanaged die roll to see if any character is
trapped. Any who are foolish enough to walk
right under the net will almost certainly be
trapped. More so, the goblins have slings and
will pelt anyone who steps out to try to undo
the net.
6 Goblins: AC 14, HD 1-1, #At 1 shortsword or 
1 sling, Dam 1d6 shortsword, 1d4 sling, Mv 20', 
Sv F1, ML 7, XP 25 ea.
HP 3 ☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
3 ☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐

areas.description: A trade town at the crossroads of a river and an old paved highway. It offers smithies, merchants, healer/cleric services, horses, wagons, freight, coach and courier services, and docks with a wooden pier and shed where boats may be bought or rented. Most of the town is enclosed by an irregular 15' wooden stockade being replaced by a 15' high, 10' thick stone wall with battlements. Woods to the northwest are being cleared for expansion. East, north, and south gates are heavy wooden gates with a nearby watch hut for three soldiers each. The east gate leads to farms and forest; a cobblestone highway runs north-south through town. There is also a common orchard with apples, pears, pecans, etc.
Hunters and dog breeders residence with several large pens for hunting and guard dogs.
Chicken ranchers and home. A family raising numerous chickens for eggs, meat, and feathers, with coops and a fenced run the chickens can retreat to.
A heavily built two-story family residence of large oaken beams, with the upper story overhanging the lower. The occupants do odd jobs for a living.
Smithy. The smith and two apprentices tend the forge, making nails, tacks, and a variety of iron hardware such as hinges, pokers, and railings.

General cave-region guidance: cave entrances may be placed by the GM and may be scattered. The map as drawn is wooded with clearings. Monsters avoid leaving tracks and local wildlife is scarce. Near entrances, PCs may notice guards, smoke, or cooking smells. Noisy parties outdoors alert the lairs, and monsters may sortie after dark. Underground areas are unlit unless noted, generally silent, with ceilings usually 10-15 feet high. Most room doors are heavy wooden doors with simple latches and can be barred from inside. Steps descend at roughly 1:1 grade. Elevations are intentionally irregular and approximate; tunnels constantly slope up and down.
Orc Lair A. These orcs share guarding duty for the local orc temple with lair B. They are brash, haughty, cruel, tricky, and nasty.
Entrance to Orc Lair A. A gaping entranceway with two orcs usually standing outside on guard; others are in the first chamber, their usual bunk room.
Empty room in Orc Lair A. The walls and some of the ceiling collapsed some time ago, making it unfit to live in.
Common room in Orc Lair A used as quarters for a number of orcs, with crude furniture in the center, wooden bunks lining the walls, and sacks of foodstuffs and hanging meat in the rear.
treasure: 12-72 pounds each of vegetables and meat

areas.description: Wolf pen. Both ends are blocked by full-height wooden fences with narrow rope-lashed gates. Inside are three wolves. The soft earthen floor is littered with wooden water bowls, bones, and offal. The wolves are usually alert and their barking/howling may attract wandering orcs or the jailers in room 5.

Door to jail and jail area. The outer room is the quarters of the big, sweaty orc jail warden and two assistants, containing crude furniture and simple wooden or stone interrogation devices. The key to the jail door is usually hung on a hide cord on the wall. The locked eastern door opens into the jail, where captives are held. Currently a bedraggled merchant missing a finger is imprisoned there.
treasure: Rescued merchant rewards party with 100 gp from his home

areas.description: Chief's room in Orc Lair A. The strong, shrewd orc leader sits on a large heavily built throne when judging court. A southern sub-room contains a bed and personal furniture. His pets are a mountain lion with a gemmed collar and two wolves, usually chained; four orcs are usually present as guards and servants. The chief has the key to the west temple door in room 9.
treasure: Gemmed collar on mountain lion worth 100 gp in gems
Silk pillow on throne worth 200 gp
Under throne seat: 671 gp, 1,235 sp, 785 cp
Potion of Passwall in a stoppered flask inside a padded jar

areas.description: Common room/guard room in Orc Lair A. Several orcs are housed here as a guard post against those in lair B. The room is cluttered with crude furniture, common foodstuffs in bags, and dried meats hanging on strings.
Pools fed by underground springs, providing a year-round source of fresh water. They are clean, 6' deep, and otherwise unremarkable.

Shared hallway with a heavy thick wooden door at each end, bound in steel and fitted with heavy locks. Each orc chief (rooms 6 and 13) has the key to the opposite side, so they must cooperate to enter the temple. The temple guards on either side were locked out by a high priest after infighting.

Orc temple cut from living stone and well maintained. It has four fluted ornate pillars, a floor tiled in polished green and red marble, and a 20' high ceiling. On a dais 6' above the floor stands a 12' black basalt statue of a vindictive evil orc god. No steps lead onto the dais.
treasure: Two red gem eyes in statue, each worth 250 gp

areas.description: Orc Lair B. These orcs share temple-guarding duty with lair A and have a cruel sense of humor.
Entrance chamber of Orc Lair B. Four orcs are immediately inside along with a hobgoblin who joined up with them looking for greener pastures.

Secret safe room known only to the orc chief in room 13. Both secret doors are extremely well hidden and trapped: opening without disarming releases sleep gas in a 10' area and sets off a loud alarm for a minute in the chief's quarters. The chamber contains emergency supplies.
Guard post at a door with a short corridor beyond acting as a buffer zone in case of attack.

Chief's quarters in Orc Lair B. The wily orc chief lives here with a war leader and two common orcs. The chief wears the key to the eastern temple door at room 9 on a necklace of pencil-thin gold bars. Treasure is kept in a locked chest with a lethal gas trap that can be disarmed using the necklace. An alarm bell under a heavy chair rings if either trapped secret door in room 11 is opened without disarming it first.
treasure: Necklace of pencil-thin gold bars worth 100 gp
Chest contains 800 gp, 500 ep, 700 sp, Potion of Healing

areas.description: Common room in Orc Lair B. Trash-littered room housing nine orcs, currently amusing themselves by throwing rocks at one of their number while others are bruised.

Common room in Orc Lair B housing seven orcs. It is cluttered with debris they have torn up and discarded, plus firewood and stone cooking circles. A hidden cache lies in the northeast corner beneath fireplace rocks and a foot of earth.
treasure: Hidden bag with 10 pp and 400 sp

areas.description: Hobgoblin Lair. The hobgoblins have taken over what was intended as an expansion of the funeral crypt at D and are renovating it. The interior mixes smooth finished stone, rough unfinished work, and hobgoblin modifications.

Twin entrances lead into the first chamber where five hobgoblins are bunked on guard duty. At point A is a wide underground stream used as a moat; crossing planks are stored on the opposite side and an ox-horn can be blown to summon hobgoblins from room 17 to place them. Other crossing methods are possible. At point C is a crude hide painted to resemble stone for ambushes.

Trapped hallway with three sets of pillars supporting a 20' ceiling. Thin wires strung between the center pillars trigger a heavy swinging stone-headed mallet, followed by a bell-covered net that entangles victims and makes loud noise. Hobgoblins from room 17 may hear and investigate.

Common room in the Hobgoblin Lair where six hobgoblins bunk. They are usually unarmored unless given time. The space has been fixed up with former casket platforms used as benches and tables. Three elaborate red funeral shrouds are used as hammocks.
treasure: Three red funeral shrouds worth 50 gp each

areas.description: Store room, usually locked but poorly secured. The chief in room 21 has the key. It is packed with odds and ends and minor plunder such as clothing, grain, potatoes, dried meats, perhaps slain foes hanging as 'fresh meat,' plus mining tools.

Sub-leader's room taken over by a skilled tough hobgoblin who lives in luxury. A short bow and two quivers hang on the wall among other possessions. He carries the key to this room on a hair string on his belt.
Guard room where hobgoblins have piled rubble in the hallway and stockpiled holy water to fend off the ghouls to the west.
Chief's room containing the hobgoblin chief and three bodyguards. The chief has the key to store room 18. Furnishings include a plain bed, chairs, tables, and other goods.
treasure: Potion of Stone to Flesh (1 use)
Bag tied to bed with 600 gp, 1,200 sp, 1,800 cp

areas.description: Abandoned Crypts. Old funeral crypts once meant to be expanded toward lair C. The entrance facade is ruined, much marble facing broken away, and the name plaque shattered. Bits of bone, scraps of cloth, and chunks of wood litter the floors. There are also five small rounded alcoves along the walls with shrine pedestals.
Area where three ghouls lurk, trying to get through the rubble to reach the hobgoblins in room 20. They are not interested in skeletons or zombies as food.
Space containing six skeletons, three on the east and three on the west, standing like guards at attention and attacking intruders on sight. The area is cluttered with broken coffins.
Area with zombies waiting under orders to attack and destroy intruders. Contains empty or smashed old coffins and the splinters of what appears to have been an ornate cabinet.
treasure: Six intact vials of holy water

areas.description: Skeleton post where skeletons attack on sight. The outward-facing walls are lined with crypt spaces, many empty. Occupied niches contain poor burials, but one visible crypt niche holds a valuable gem necklace hanging out in plain sight.
treasure: 26 gp and 49 sp total in crypt spaces
If returned to original owners, necklace earns 1,000 gp reward

areas.description: Goblin Lair. Goblins live here in a simple, dark, dank, crudely tunneled warren. Many halls are 6' to 8' wide and equally high. They often serve as expendable troops for stronger tribes.

Guard post in the Goblin Lair. Six goblins wait to fight intruders and control a rope-dropped net trap covering the marked entrance area. Entangled victims are vulnerable and goblins use slings against anyone trying to free them.
